_description: Adapts the invoice_complex domain model to the reactive pattern with proper app structure, modules, users, roles, and authorization context. Demonstrates reactive computation cascades, event bindings, data schemas, and artifact definitions.

description.en: Days until due
Enumeration of invoice lifecycle statuses
A company that provides goods or services
A commercial document issued by a vendor for goods or services rendered
A line item on an invoice representing a specific service or product
Aggregated sales data across dimensions like service category, vendor, and year
Relationship between an invoice and its vendor
Composition relationship between an invoice and its line items
Date the invoice was issued (ISO 8601)
Date payment is due
Current status: DRAFT, SUBMITTED, UNDER_REVIEW, APPROVED, REJECTED, PAID
Payment terms: Net15, Net30, Net60, Net90, DueOnReceipt
Invoice currency code
Year the invoice was issued (for temporal aggregation)
Sum of all line items before tax (computed)
Total tax on the invoice (computed as 8.5% of subtotal)
Total amount including tax (computed)
Validation flag computed from schema-based data extraction (1=valid, 0=invalid)
Additional notes or comments
Category of the service or product
Computed: quantity * unit_price
Tax rate percentage for this item
Computed: line_total * (tax_rate / 100)
Service category dimension for sales aggregation
Vendor dimension for sales aggregation
Sum of line totals for aggregated items
Sum of tax amounts for aggregated items
Count of items in this aggregation
Average unit price of aggregated items
Tracks whether high-sales threshold has been crossed
Year dimension for temporal sales aggregation
Event emitted when a new invoice is created
Event emitted when an invoice is approved for payment
Event emitted when an invoice is paid
Event emitted when an invoice becomes overdue
Event emitted when an invoice total has been computed via reactive cascade
Event emitted when category sales exceed $50K threshold
Application for managing vendor invoices with reactive computations, event-driven workflows, and role-based authorization
Core invoicing operations: CRUD on invoices, items, and vendors. Publishes creation and computation events.
Invoice review and approval workflow. Subscribes to computation events and publishes approval decisions.

computations._comment: === COMPUTATION 1: Item Line Total = quantity * unit_price ===

description.en: Computes line_total = quantity * unit_price for each invoice item

computations.formula: let qty = quantity.value; let price = unit_price.value; qty * price

computations._comment: === COMPUTATION 2: Invoice Subtotal = Sum(items.line_total) via role_aggregate ===

description.en: Aggregates line_total from all items linked via ai-invoice-item-rel
Computes tax as 8.5% of subtotal
Computes total = subtotal + tax_amount

computations.formula: let sub = subtotal.value; let t = tax.value; sub + t

description.en: Computes tax_amount = line_total * (tax_rate / 100)

computations.formula: let lt = line_total.value; let rate = if tax_rate.value > 0.0 { tax_rate.value } else { 8.5 }; lt * rate / 100.0

description.en: Validates invoice data using schema-based extraction: checks item_count > 0 and total > 0

computations.formula: let data = invoice_data; let item_count = data.line_items.len(); let is_valid = item_count > 0 && data.total > 0.0; if is_valid { 1.0 } else { 0.0 }

description.en: Publishes ai-invoice-total-computed event when invoice total changes

computations.formula: print(`[EVENT EMIT] Invoice total computed: $${total.value}`); total.value

description.en: Subscribes to ai-invoice-total-computed events via TopicMessage trigger and logs receipt

computations.formula: print(`[EVENT RECEIVED] Invoice total event received - Invoice ID: ${event.invoice_id}, Total: $${event.total_amount}`); 0.0

description.en: Complete invoice with nested vendor and line items
Simplified schema for invoice validation - total and line items only

artifact_definitions._comment: === ARTIFACT 1: Sales by Service Category (with threshold) ===
artifact_definitions.name: Sales by Service Category

description.en: Aggregates invoice item sales by service category with $50K high-sales threshold
Aggregates item sales by vendor, navigating item -> invoice -> vendor via RelatorPath
Annual sales aggregation - navigates item -> invoice via RelatorPath to get year

_future_app_roles_comment._note: These AppRoleDefinition objects document the intended fine-grained app roles for when the metadata loader supports app_role_definitions. Currently mapped to foundation roles: account-administrator=AppAdmin, account-reviewer=AppUser, account-specialist=AppDeveloper.

description.en: Full app management: configuration, user roles, and all CRUD operations
Read-only access to invoices with approve/reject workflow capability
Full CRUD on invoices, items, and vendors
